test.name: Structured Output Compliance
test.description: Produce JSON matching an exact schema with no extra fields
test.prompt:
List exactly 7 programming languages that were first released after the year 2010.

You MUST follow this exact format for each item:
<number>. <LanguageName> (<year>) - <one sentence description>

Rules:
1. List EXACTLY 7 languages — not 6, not 8.
2. Each language must have been first released (v1.0 or initial public release) AFTER 2010, meaning 2011 or later.
3. Each item must be on its own line.
4. The format must be exactly: number, period, space, language name, space, year in parentheses, space, dash, space, one sentence description.
5. Each description must be exactly ONE sentence (one period at the end, no additional sentences).
6. Do NOT include any text before the list (no introduction, no "Here are..." preamble).
7. Do NOT include any text after the list (no summary, no notes, no caveats).
8. Output ONLY the 7 numbered items, nothing else.

Example of the correct format (do NOT use this language, it's just to show format):
1. ExampleLang (2015) - A language designed for example purposes only.

claude-opus-4-8.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language for the JVM developed by JetBrains and widely used for Android development.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created by Google that powers the Flutter UI framework.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang virtual machine for scalable applications.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language designed primarily for numerical and scientific computing.
6. Swift (2014) - A language created by Apple for building iOS, macOS, and other Apple platform applications.
7. Crystal (2014) - A compiled language with Ruby-inspired syntax that offers static type checking and high performance.

claude-sonnet-4-6.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed, JVM-based language developed by JetBrains that is fully interoperable with Java and widely used for Android development.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized, object-oriented language developed by Google for building fast, cross-platform applications using the Flutter framework.
3. Elixir (2011) - A functional, concurrent language built on the Erlang VM designed for building scalable, fault-tolerant distributed systems.
4. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript and improves developer tooling at scale.
5. Julia (2012) - A high-performance dynamic language designed specifically for numerical and scientific computing with syntax familiar to mathematicians.
6. Swift (2014) - An open-source, compiled language developed by Apple as a modern, safe replacement for Objective-C for building Apple platform applications.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed, compiled language with Ruby-inspired syntax that aims to deliver the readability of Ruby with the performance of C.

gpt-5-5.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed language designed for concise, interoperable development on the JVM and beyond.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized language created for building web, server, and mobile applications.
3. TypeScript (2012) - A typed superset of JavaScript that adds static checking for large-scale application development.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-performance language designed for numerical computing, data science, and scientific programming.
5. Swift (2014) - A modern systems and application language created by Apple for safe and fast software development.
6. Hack (2014) - A gradually typed language derived from PHP and designed for reliable web application development.
7. Crystal (2014) - A compiled language with Ruby-inspired syntax and static type inference for fast native programs.

grok.modelOutput:
1. Kotlin (2011) - A statically typed programming language developed by JetBrains that targets the Java Virtual Machine and is interoperable with Java.
2. Dart (2011) - A client-optimized open-source programming language developed by Google for building web, mobile, and server applications.
3. Elixir (2012) - A functional concurrent programming language built on the Erlang virtual machine and known for scalability and fault tolerance.
4. Julia (2012) - A high-level dynamic programming language designed for high-performance numerical and scientific computing.
5. TypeScript (2012) - A statically typed superset of JavaScript developed by Microsoft that compiles to plain JavaScript.
6. Swift (2014) - A compiled multi-paradigm programming language developed by Apple primarily for iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms.
7. Crystal (2014) - A statically typed object-oriented programming language with Ruby-like syntax that compiles to efficient native code.
